protein_name: Plays a role in the formation of clathrin-coated invaginations and endocytosis. Belongs to the epsin family. Highest expression is found in brain. Detected at lower levels in lung and liver. 4 alternatively spliced human isoforms have been reported. Note: This description may include information from UniProtKB.

raw_fields.Cellular Component: clathrin vesicle coat; clathrin-coated vesicle; cytoplasm; cytoplasmic vesicle; cytosol; endosome; plasma membrane
raw_fields.Molecular Function: clathrin binding; lipid binding; phospholipid binding; protein binding
raw_fields.Biological Process: endocytosis; negative regulation of sprouting angiogenesis; negative regulation of vascular endothelial growth factor receptor signaling pathway; positive regulation of Notch signaling pathway
raw_fields.Alt. Names/Synonyms: EHB21; EPN2; EPS-15-interacting protein 2; Eps15 binding protein; epsin 2; Epsin-2; KIAA1065
raw_fields.Basal Isoelectric point: 7.11 Predict pI for various phosphorylation states
